text.title: AI-Pair Onboarding Script Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules with rationale + source for ai-pair-onboarding-script. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Script MUST detect installed AI agent CLIs (`claude`, `cursor`, `gh copilot`) and check version against the team's minimum-version pin.
rule.rationale: Old CLI versions miss tool support; pinning is the only deterministic check.

rule.statement: Script MUST verify `AGENTS.md` exists at repo root and matches the canonical team template (checksum, not full match — local additions allowed).
rule.rationale: Without AGENTS.md the agent has no project context; onboarding cannot finish.

rule.statement: Script MUST verify `.claude/settings.json` exists and `allowedTools` is the team's minimum set, not `*`.
rule.rationale: Defaulting to all tools is the #1 onboarding mistake; bake the policy into the bootstrap.

rule.statement: Script MUST run a smoke task ('describe the build system in 3 bullets') and confirm the agent returns a non-empty response referencing files actually in the repo.

rationale.source: Catches API key misconfiguration, network egress block, or wrong model

rule.rationale: Catches API key misconfiguration, network egress block, or wrong model — issues that surface mid-task otherwise.

rule.statement: Script MUST emit a JSON report with `ready_to_pair: true|false`, signed by the local user, written to `~/.faion/onboarding-report.json` for audit.
rule.rationale: Audit trail proves who completed onboarding; resolves 'I never ran it' disputes.

rule.statement: If the engineer is a <1-week rotation, or the team has no standardised AI-pair workflow, return `skipped`.
